channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Global warming fills New England's rich waters with death traps for endangered sea turtles
item.description: As global warming fills the plankton-rich waters of New England with death traps for sea turtles, the number of stranded reptiles has multiplied over the last 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Rodrique Ngowi And Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk's Neuralink expands offices in Austin area
item.description: Musk's brain-computer interface company, Neuralink, will expand in the area with more space and job openings.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: China has announced a ban on exports to the United States of gallium, germanium and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Here’s how 2 European search upstarts aim to go after Google—from the index up
item.description: Google’s dominance of web search—consistently above 85% of the market in the U.S.—is an unmissable fact of online life.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Bipartisan bill would study AI's impact on banking, housing
item.description: A new bill with bipartisan support would commission studies on the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the banking and housing sectors, where lawmakers have been sounding an alarm about algorithmic price fixing and commercial misconduct enabled by new technologies.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, The Hill

item.title: Panasonic revives its late founder as AI
item.description: Japanese electronics company Panasonic has created an AI version of its founder, Kōnosuke Matsushita, who died in 1989, The Japan Times reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS PartyRock updated with free daily usage for developers
item.description: Amazon Web Services has updated AWS PartyRock, its mostly free low code tool for building generative AI applications with a new app search function and the ability to build document processing into applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data broker blunder: More than 600,000 sensitive files exposed in data services leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how 600,000-plus records, including checks and property data, were left exposed online by an information service provider.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is your friend's phone number compromised? Here's what to look for
item.description: That weird text from a friend may actually be from a hacker. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson tells you what signs to look out for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2025 will be a bad year for remote work
item.description: “Mr. Musk Goes to Washington.” And he’s planning to roll back remote work in the federal government. Tech entrepreneur Elon Musk and one-time presidential candidate Vivek Ramaswamy are expected to be appointed by incoming president Donald J.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Refactoring AI code: The good, the bad, and the weird
item.description: Generative AI is no longer a novelty in the software development world: it’s being increasingly used as an assistant (and sometimes a free agent) to write code running in real-world production. But every developer knows that writing new code from scratch is only a small part of their daily work.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bitcoin flirts with $100K as crypto industry rallies for Trump
item.description: Bitcoin has blasted through a series of record highs since President-elect Trump’s election victory as the crypto industry anticipates a significant shift in federal policy.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Two years of ChatGPT: the conversation that never ends
item.description: Two years can be an eternity for the technology industry: plenty of time for enterprises to innovate, launch a new product, peak on the stock market… and then plummet again. Think about the bubbles that briefly surrounded 3D printing, smart glasses, or the metaverse.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes
item.description: The directive builds upon a mandate issued in February — the public notice doesn’t disclose specifics of the new requirements. The post Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Windows Recall: All your privacy questions answered
item.description: Windows Recall is an AI-based feature that will be built into new Copilot+ PCs. Recall takes snapshots of your screen every few seconds and uses that data to make a searchable index of everything you’ve ever done on your PC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
